query: European Commission Temu action plan deadline August 28 compliance review period

mako.raw:
{"query":"European Commission Temu action plan deadline August 28 compliance review period","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1446,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://thelens.slaughterandmay.com/post/102n15m/european-commission-fines-temu-200-million-under-the-dsa","title":"European Commission Fines Temu €200 Million Under the DSA, Catherine O'Callaghan, Laura Houston, Will Manley, Natalie Donovan","content":"The decision, published on 28 May 2026, marks one of the most significant enforcement actions under the DSA to date and highlights the Commission's evolving expectations regarding the services designated as “Very Large Online Platforms” under the Act.
The €200 million fine is only the second penalty imposed by the Commission for DSA infringements, and it is the largest issued to date (the first being the €120 million fine issued to X in December 2025).
After this, the Commission will decide whether the action plan is sufficient to resolve the infringements, and will set a reasonable period for implementation."},{"url":"https://digitalpolicyalert.org/change/10160-european-commission-investigation-into-temus-compliance-with-digital-service-acts-requirement-on-establishment-of-notice-and-action-mechanism","title":"European Commission investigation into Temu's compliance with Digital Service Act's requirements - Digital Policy Alert","content":"On 28 May 2026, the European Commission issued a non-compliance decision fining Temu EUR 200 million for breaching Digital Services Act (DSA) systemic risk assessment obligations.
On 31 October 2024, the European Commission opened a formal procedure to assess whether Temu may have infringed the Digital Service Act (DSA).
The European Commission aims to assess compliance with the DSA’s obligation to address and mitig…
On 28 June 2024, the European Commission issued a formal request for information to the online marketplace Temu, under the Digital Services Act (DSA)."},{"url":"https://prodlaw.eu/2026/06/understanding-temu-fine-under-the-eu-digital-services-act/","title":"TEMU fine under the EU Digital Services Act","content":"In May 2026, the European Commission issued a 200 million euros fine to TEMU, a large Chinese online marketplace which counts about 130 million users in the EU.
Under the DSA, very large online platforms are required to assess risks linked to their services and adopt corresponding mitigation measures.
TEMU has time until 28 August to submit an action plan to the Commission to address these breaches."},{"url":"https://www.lewissilkin.com/en/insights/2026/06/02/european-commission-fines-temu-200-million-for-breaching-the-digital-services-ac-102n0sa","title":"European Commission fines Temu €200 million for breaching the Digital Services Act","content":"The decision follows an investigation opened in 2024 and preliminary findings issued in July 2025.
The Commission's mystery shopping exercise found that many selected chargers failed basic safety tests and that many tested baby toys posed medium to high safety risks, including excessive chemicals and detachable parts that created suffocation hazards.
In setting the fine, the Commission took account of the nature, gravity and duration of the infringement, describing failures to conduct proper risk assessments as a particularly serious breach because risk assessment is one of the cornerstones of the DSA framework."},{"url":"https://iclg.com/news/temu-falls-foul-of-european-commission/","title":"Temu falls foul of European Commission | ICLG","content":"According to data from Backlinko, as of January 2026, Temu had 416.5 million monthly active users worldwide, of which 141.6 million were from the EU.
In February 2025, the European Consumer Organisation (BCEU) published the results of tests into Temu’s product offerings carried out across Denmark, Italy and the UK over a 15-month period.
Temu now has until 28 August 2026 to put an action plan setting out rectifying measures before the Commission.
The European Board for Digital Services will issue an opinion one month after the receipt of the action plan, following which the Commission will have one month to adopt a final decision and set a reasonable implementation period."}]}

We have shared today our concerns","score":0.644248,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.dataguidance.com/news/eu-commission-fines-temu-eur200-million-dsa-risk","title":"EU: Commission fines Temu €200M for DSA risk assessment failures | News | DataGuidance","content":"*   **Fine Imposed**: The European Commission fined Temu €200 million for failing to meet Digital Services Act (DSA) risk assessment standards. *   **Risk Assessment Failures**: Temu's risk assessment relied on generic assumptions, underestimated consumer exposure to illegal products, and overlooked evidence from enforcement actions. Temu was fined for failing to properly identify, analyze, and assess systemic risks linked to the sale of illegal products on its platform. What did Temu's risk assessment fail to meet according to the Commission? Temu's risk assessment failed to meet DSA standards because it relied on generic e-commerce risk assumptions, underestimated the likelihood of encountering illegal products, and overlooked concrete evidence from enforcement actions. The Commission found that Temu failed to properly identify, analyze, and assess systemic risks linked to the sale of illegal products on its platform, exposing EU consumers to significant harm.BackgroundThe Commission opened formal proceedings against Temu on October 31, 2024, issued preliminary findings in July 2025, and closed the case with a noncompliance decision on May 28, 2026. https://www.onetrust.com/privacy/. https://www.onetrust.com/cookie-policy/.","score":0.4237686,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://commission.europa.eu/topics/consumers/consumer-rights-and-complaints/enforcement-consumer-protection/coordinated-actions/market-places-and-digital-services_en","title":"Market places and digital services - European Commission","content":"In May 2025, the CPC Network notified the online marketplace and e-retailer SHEIN of a number of practices on its platform that, according to the network’s assessment infringe EU consumer law. The network directed SHEIN to bring those practices in line with consumer laws of the European Union. SHEIN remains under investigation and was requested to provide further information to the CPC Network. The CPC Network’s coordinated investigation covers a broad range of practices with which consumers are confronted while shopping on SHEIN and that the network considers to be in breach of EU consumer protection laws. In addition, the CPC Network requested information from SHEIN to assess its compliance with further obligations under EU consumer law, such as the obligation to ensure that product rankings, reviews, and ratings are not presented to consumers in a misleading manner. The CPC Network’s coordinated investigation covers a broad range of practices with which consumers are confronted while shopping on Temu, that the network considers to be in breach of EU consumer protection laws.","score":0.37572992,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.82,"request_id":"069878d9-374f-414b-8fff-457dbb562a74"}
